title: Blacklisting in public contracts: Navigating legal challenges and judicial scrutiny
description: In government contracts, a blacklisting order implies banning a contractor from participating in future tenders or projects. It is employed by government entities to safeguard public interest and ensure that only reliable and capable contractors are allotted public works. However, the consequences of blacklisting can be severe for the blacklisted entity, often described by courts as the contractor’s “civil death”.The severity of blacklisting stems from the fact that most government entities mand
